response:
Section 1  
Write a Python function to count the number of occurrences of a specific word in a given text, ignoring case sensitivity. The input will be a string containing the text and another string containing the word to search for. The function should return an integer representing how many times the specified word appears in the text.  

Section 2  
The function should be case insensitive, so "Freedom" and "freedom" should be counted as the same word. The function should return an integer representing the count of the word in the text.  

Section 3  
The function should handle punctuation and other non-alphanumeric characters by ignoring them. The function should also handle multiple spaces between words and ignore any extra spaces.  

Section 4  
The function should be efficient and handle large inputs without performance issues. The function should return the correct count and handle edge cases such as empty text or empty word.  

P.S.: Your response must have 4 sections. Mark the beginning of each section with Section X, such as:  
Section 1  
[content of section 1]  
Section 2  
[content of section 2]  
No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet.
